analogy: Think of "Recent Developments in Indian Politics" through the chapter's own opening images: In this chapter… In this last chapter we take a synoptic view of the last two decades of politics in India. These developments are complex, for various kinds of factors came together to produce unanticipated outcomes in this period. The new era in politics was... The easy idea is to follow the chapter's own clues one by one instead of memorising a loose definition. First notice the words and examples the text repeats: 138 Politics in India since Independence, 1 May 1996 25 October 1995 20 August 2001 25 October 2004, 140 Politics in India since Independence, 142 Politics in India since Independence. Then connect each example to the question the chapter is trying to answer. Like solving a source-backed puzzle, every useful point must come from the chapter text itself and stay traceable to the NCERT source.
full_notes:
Recent Developments in Indian Politics is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins with its own framing, examples and questions, then builds the topic through source-backed facts. The notes below keep that order: first the opening problem, then the important terms, then the mechanisms, examples and exam-useful connections that appear in the chapter itself.

The text explains that in this chapter… In this last chapter we take a synoptic view of the last two decades of politics in India.

The text explains that these developments are complex, for various kinds of factors came together to produce unanticipated outcomes in this period.

The text explains that the new era in politics was impossible to foresee; it is still very difficult to understand.

The text explains that these developments are also controversial, for these involve deep conflicts and we are still too close to the events.

The text explains that yet we can ask some questions central to the political change in this period. • What are the implications of the rise of coalition politics for our democracy? • What is Mandalisation all about?

The text explains that in which ways will it change the nature of political representation? • What is the legacy of the Ram Janmabhoomi movement? • What does the rise of a new policy consensus do to the nature of political choices?

The text explains that it simply gives you the necessary information and some tools so that you can ask and answer these questions when you are through with this book.

The text explains that we cannot avoid asking these questions just because they are politically sensitive, for the whole point of studying the history of politics in India since Independence is to make sense of our present.

The text explains that ups and downs of various political parties in the 1990s appeared to many, like this cartoon drawn in 1990, as a roller coaster ride.

The text explains that riding the roller coaster are Rajiv Gandhi, V.

The text explains that context of the 1990s You have read in the last chapter that Rajiv Gandhi became the Prime Minister after the assassination of Indira Gandhi.

The text explains that he led the Congress to a massive victory in the Lok Sabha elections held immediately thereafter in 1984.

The text explains that as the decade of the eighties came to a close, the country witnessed five developments that were to make a long-lasting impact on our politics.

The text explains that f irst the most crucial development of this period was the defeat of the Congress party in the elections held in 1989.

The text explains that the party that had won as many as 415 seats in the Lok Sabha in 1984 was reduced to only 197 in this election.

The text explains that the Congress improved its performance and came back to power soon after the mid-term elections held in 1991.

The text explains that but the elections of 1989 marked the end of what political scientists have called the ‘Congress system’.

The text explains that but it lost the kind of centrality it earlier enjoyed in the party system.

The text explains that this followed the decision by the new National Front government in 1990, to implement the recommendation of the Mandal Commission that jobs in central government should be reserved for the Other Backward Classes.

The text explains that congress leader Sitaram Kesri withdrew the crutches of support from Deve Gowda’s United Front Government.

The text explains that 8chapter recent Developments in inDian politics

The text explains that 138 Politics in India since Independence Third, the economic policy followed by the various governments took a radically different turn.

The text explains that started by Rajiv Gandhi, these changes first became very visible in 1991 and radically changed the direction that the Indian economy had pursued since Independence.

The text explains that but the various governments that came to power in this period have continued to follow these.

The text explains that the Ram Janmabhoomi Temple Movement, becoming the central issue, transformed the direction of the discourse on secularism and democracy.

The text explains that these changes culminated in the construction of the Ram Temple at Ayodhya following the decision of the constitutional bench of the Supreme Court (which was announced on November 9, 2019).

The text explains that on this basis, the National Front formed a coalition government, but the BJP and the Left Front did not join in this government.

The text explains that decline of Congress The defeat of the Congress party marked the end of Congress dominance over the Indian party system.

The text explains that do you remember the discussion in Chapters about the restoration of the Congress system?

The text explains that recent Developments in Indian Politics 141 Thus, began an era of multi-party system.

The text explains that this development initiated an era of coalition governments at the Centre, in which regional parties played a crucial role in forming ruling alliances but in 2014 and 2019 Lok Sabha election, the BJP got clear majority on its own.

The text explains that sit together in groups and draw a comprehensive list of the events reported by your parents, see which events get cited most, and compare them with what the chapter suggests were the most significant.

The text explains that these parties played an important role in the United Front government that came to power in 1996.

The text explains that this time the BJP did not support the government.

The text explains that the United Front government was supported by the Congress.

The text explains that in 1989, both the Left and the BJP supported the National Front Government because they wanted to keep the Congress out of power.

The text explains that in 1996, the Left continued to support the non-Congress government but this time the Congress, supported it, as both the Congress and the Left wanted to keep the BJP out of power.

The text explains that it emerged as the largest party in the 1996 election and was invited to form the government.

The text explains that but most other parties were opposed to its policies and therefore, the BJP government could not secure a majority in the Lok Sabha.

The text explains that 142 Politics in India since Independence a coalition government from May 1998 to June 1999 and was re-elected in October 1999.

The text explains that atal Behari Vajpayee was the Prime Minister during both these NDA governments and his government formed in 1999 completed its full term.

The text explains that since then, there have been eleven governments at the Centre, all of which have either been coalition governments or minority governments supported by other parties, which did not join the government.

The text explains that in this new phase, any government could be formed only with the participation or support of many regional parties.

The text explains that the era of coalition governments may be seen as a long-term trend resulting from relatively silent changes that were taking place over the last few decades.

The text explains that we saw in Chapter Two that in earlier times, it was the Congress party itself that was a ‘coalition’ of different interests and different social strata and groups.

The text explains that this gave rise to the term ‘Congress system’.

The text explains that a cartoonist’s depiction of the change from one-party dominance to a multi-party alliance system.

The text explains that not only has the Congress system destroyed itself, but the fragmentation of the Congress coalition has triggered a new emphasis on self-representation which raises questions about the party system and its capacity to accommodate diverse interests, …. .

The text explains that an important test facing the polity is to evolve a party system or political parties that can effectively articulate and aggregate a variety of interests. — Zoya Hasan (a) Write a short note on what the author calls challenges of the party system in the light of what you have read in this chapter.

The text explains that (b) Given an example from this chapter of the lack of accommodation and aggregation mentioned in this passage.

The text explains that (c) Why is it necessary for parties to accommodate and aggregate variety of interests?

The text explains that recent Developments in Indian Politics 157 LET US DO IT TOGETHER • This chapter covers the major developments in Indian politics until the 2004 Elections (14th Lok Sabha).

Source: NCERT Class 12 Politics in India since Independence, Chapter 8: Recent Developments in Indian Politics

Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
